onboarding.heading: Guided Setup
onboarding.step1_description: Required to read notifications. This opens the Android system settings menu.
onboarding.step2_description: Point NotifyBridge at your Mosquitto / Home Assistant MQTT broker.
onboarding.step3_description: Nothing is forwarded until you pick apps. Your choice, your privacy.
onboarding.privacy_body: Everything stays on your network. No cloud, no Google, no Firebase.

status.queued_notifications: {count, plural, one {# queued notification} other {# queued notifications}}
status.outbox_buffer_note: buffers automatically if broker goes away
status.apps_enabled: {count, plural, one {# app enabled} other {# apps enabled}}

broker.title: Broker
broker.field_device_supporting: used in the MQTT topic and Home Assistant entity
broker.pinned_cert_note: Pinned cert verifies your self-signed Mosquitto without disabling validation.

apps.title: Apps
apps.apps_forwarding_summary: {enabled} of {total, plural, one {# app} other {# apps}} forwarding
apps.banner_message: Empty by design — you opted these in. Add or remove anytime.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.notif_access_description: NotifyBridge can read posted notifications. This is the core permission.
permissions.battery_description: Android Doze can suspend the bridge while the phone is idle, delaying or dropping notifications. Exempt NotifyBridge to keep it reliable while locked.
permissions.startup_description: Re-drains buffered notifications after a reboot. Note: on Android 12+, BOOT_COMPLETED fires only after the user has opened the app at least once — documented as a known gotcha; not a defect.
permissions.applock_enable_description: Require biometric / PIN on launch and after idle
permissions.redact_description: Masks notification text in Recent — auth to reveal per item
permissions.security_note: Notification content can include OTP codes and bank alerts. NotifyBridge only forwards apps you explicitly allow, over your local network.
